{{Infobox character
| name = Zakareth
| children = {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Talos]]}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|King, [[King of Damasca]]}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|army}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|King}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|palace}}
| appears_in = Traveler's Gate
| introduced = [[House of Blades (novel)|House of Blades]]
}}

''See also: [[Zakareth Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth the Sixth]] is the [[King]] of [[Cana]] and [[Damasca]], holding the titles [[Lord]] of the Morning and Evening [[Star]]. A powerful figure with a lineage of kings, he possesses striking physical features, including one blue eye and one that burns with scarlet flame. He is the [[Incarnation of Ragnarus]], a status that grants him deep knowledge of the weapons within the [[Vault]] and the ability to perceive scarlet potential in living beings. Zakareth is a formidable combatant who utilizes weapons such as a spear and a lightning spear. His leadership is characterized by strategic aggression; he has been known to plan large-scale military strikes against territory outposts and cities. He views the people of [[Enosh]] as a dangerous cult dedicated to the [[Incarnations]] and seeks to direct power toward specific purposes. Zakareth's past actions include sealing an individual into a prison, an act he believes contributed to current instabilities. He is also involved in complex interpersonal dynamics, acting as a father to [[Leah]] and making calculated decisions regarding the treatment of [[Travelers]] based on their perceived value. Amidst rising global tensions involving the [[Incarnations]] and the gathering of [[Enosh]] for war, Zakareth remains a central figure whose control is seen as a prerequisite for the safety of others.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Zakareth serves as the [[King of Damasca]], ruling over a specific territory. His authority is so absolute that his soldiers act in accordance with his will. He possesses a red eye that potentially grants him the ability to perceive into the minds of others. As his influence grows, [[Alin]] prepares to stand in opposition to him.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">6</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Zakareth]] is the [[King]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c3">''House of Blades'', chapter 3</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Zakareth]] is the [[King of Damasca]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Zakareth]] is a figure whose will the [[Damascan soldiers]] extend.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c14">''House of Blades'', chapter 14</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Zakareth]] has a [[Territory]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c15">''House of Blades'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Alin]] intends to oppose [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Zakareth]] has a red eye that may allow him to see into minds.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c23">''House of Blades'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Zakareth is a ruler whose authority faces potential collapse should [[Valinhall]] align against [[Damasca]]. Known as a figure to whom [[Indirial]] would delegate the sending of slaves, he commands his forces while accompanied by his army. He carries a spear and wears a crown featuring a [[ruby]] capable of flaring, though his physical appearance is marked by a pale, sunken face and an eyepatch covering an empty eye socket. Throughout his reign, he places great value on the blood of Ragnarus [[Travelers]], yet the ultimate price for his strength was his own [[life]].}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">8</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] is a figure [[Indirial]] would tell to send slaves.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c1">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 1</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] is a ruler whose rule might end if [[Valinhall]] joined against [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c5">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 5</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth's crown]] has a [[ruby]] that can flare.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c15">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] values the blood of Ragnarus [[Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 16</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] is with the army.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c16" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]]'s price for his strength was his [[life]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 23</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]]'s weapon is a spear.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c23" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Zakareth]] has a pale and sunken face with an empty eye socket covered by an eyepatch.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c25">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
</ul></div>

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Initially unaware of the [[Pillar of Sunset]], Zakareth lived as a father to his son, [[Talos]], and spent time observing his people from the top of his palace. He eventually marched upon the gates of [[Cana]] supported by the [[Incarnations]] of [[Helgard]] and [[Lirial]], appearing with a [[ruby]]-headed staff, a spiked crown, and chains wrapped around his shoulders. His physical form transformed to include glowing red and gold patterns on his skin, a red right eye, and swirling crimson flames within his left eye socket. While he sought to control [[Indirial]] by turning him into an [[Incarnation]], Zakareth ultimately prepared an army and his [[Incarnations]] to attack [[Enosh]] in an effort to destroy [[Alin]] and [[Elysia]]. His reign ended when [[Simon]] decapitated him, leaving the king to believe he would return in either three hundred or a thousand years.}}

<div class="wv-facts mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"><div class="wv-facts-head mw-collapsible-toggle">Sourced facts <span class="wv-facts-count">22</span></div>
<ul class="wv-fact-list mw-collapsible-content">
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]]'s son is [[Talos]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2">''City of Light'', chapter 2</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] is a character who was unaware of the [[Pillar of Sunset]]'s existence.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c2" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] stands atop his palace and watches the people.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c11">''City of Light'', chapter 11</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] faced the gates of [[Cana]] with the [[Incarnations]] of [[Helgard]] and [[Lirial]] behind him.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c15">''City of Light'', chapter 15</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] wears a tall, spiked crown and grips a [[ruby]]-headed staff.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17">''City of Light'', chapter 17</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]]'s left eye is composed of swirling crimson flames and his right eye has turned red.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]]'s skin has patterns of red and gold that glow softly.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]]'s [[steel]] and [[Nye essence]] were completely out.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] has chains wrapped almost all the way around his shoulders.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] fears transforming into another [[Incarnation]] and threatening his family.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c17" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] wants [[Indirial]] to become an [[Incarnation]] so he can control him with the Rod.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c19">''City of Light'', chapter 19</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] has [[ruby]] and gold swirls on his skin.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c20">''City of Light'', chapter 20</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] felt an expression of Ragnarus power pulsing from the hill where [[Leah]] stood.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] does not attack [[Simon]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c22">''City of Light'', chapter 22</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Leah]] wishes [[Zakareth]] had died on the [[Vault]] floor.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c24">''City of Light'', chapter 24</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] was working for [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[King]] [[Zakareth]] is preparing to attack [[Enosh]] to destroy [[Alin]] and [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] is expected to lead with his [[Incarnations]] and an army.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c26">''City of Light'', chapter 26</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Cynara]] mentioned [[Zakareth]] in relation to her power.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27">''City of Light'', chapter 27</ref>}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] has fire in his left eye socket.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Zakareth]] believes he will return in three hundred or a thousand years.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
<li>{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Simon]] decapitated [[King]] [[Zakareth]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}</li>
</ul></div>

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"If I am to oppose Zakareth, then let me oppose him while there is still time,"<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|Maybe it will take three hundred years,” he announced. “Maybe a thousand. But I will return.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c27" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
